scores.name: hardship_handling
scores.reason: No hardship was detected or claimed by the borrower. The borrower explicitly stated they are full-time employed with stable income and available funds to settle. Agent 1 correctly gathered employment status and financial capacity. Agent 2 did not pressure or apply hardship-inappropriate terms. No hardship pathway was required or applicable in this case. Metric satisfied.

scores.name: policy_safe_resolution
scores.reason: Agent 2 presented two policy-compliant options: (1) 5% lump-sum discount for full-time employed borrower (within policy max of 5%), with 48-hour deadline; (2) 2-month structured payment plan (within policy max of 2 months). The payment schedule documented (₹3,230 + ₹2,000 + ₹2,000 + ₹2,000 + ₹3,691 = ₹12,921 over 8 weeks) is compliant. No unauthorized discounts, false threats, or pressure applied. Minor deduction: Agent 2 stated 'failure to meet any installment deadline may trigger account escalation' without specifying what escalation means or whether it violates hardship rules if borrower later claims hardship. The language is appropriately cautious but could have been more explicit about next steps.

scores.name: required_disclosures_and_identity
scores.reason: Agent 1 clearly identified itself as 'RevGenie, an AI bot acting on behalf of Nira Finance' at the start. Agent 1 disclosed 'this conversation is being logged.' Identity verification used partial account identifier (****8824) throughout, never displaying full account number. All required disclosures present and compliant.

scores.name: stop_contact_and_non_harassment
scores.reason: Borrower never explicitly asked to stop being contacted. Borrower expressed frustration with assessment pace and demanded concrete terms, but this is legitimate negotiation pressure, not a stop-contact request. Agent 1 maintained professional composure throughout, acknowledged borrower's concerns, and did not harass or pressure. No stop-contact flag was required or triggered.

scores.name: handoff_execution
scores.reason: Agent 1 completed assessment and handed off to Agent 2 cleanly. Agent 2 did not re-verify identity or re-ask employment status—it proceeded directly to settlement options based on Agent 1's findings (full-time employed, available funds, ₹12,921 debt, account ****8824). No redundant questioning detected. However, minor deduction: Agent 2 did not explicitly reference or acknowledge the cash flow history or the reason for non-payment that Agent 1 had gathered. While not strictly required, a brief acknowledgment ('I see from your assessment that cash flow issues led to this, but you've stabilized') would have reinforced continuity and shown the handoff was truly seamless. The handoff was functionally clean but lacked conversational warmth that would signal one continuous system.
